rules.name: competitor_brand_naming_ban
rules.description: Prevents naming direct tyre retail or fitting competitors by name in ad copy
rules.rationale: Naming competitors in paid ads can trigger legal challenges, inflate competitor brand awareness at Tyroola's expense, and violate platform advertising policies. Generic terms like 'other retailers' are preferred. Note: 'just tyres' as a phrase (e.g. 'No salesman, just tyres') is Tyroola's own copy — only the brand name 'JustTyres' (one word) is blocked.

rules.name: incorrect_service_model_claims
rules.description: Prevents claims that Tyroola operates physical tyre shops, walk-in stores, or traditional fitment centres
rules.rationale: Tyroola is a pure online retailer with mobile fitters. Implying a physical shopfront is factually incorrect, misleads consumers, and could constitute false advertising under Australian Consumer Law.

rules.name: banned_industry_terms
rules.description: Prevents use of banned, misleading, or legally risky terms common in the automotive tyre industry
rules.rationale: Superlative pricing claims require substantiation under ACCC guidelines. Safety-absolute claims are unverifiable and potentially dangerous. These terms expose Tyroola to regulatory action and consumer complaints.

rules.name: headline_title_case_ban
rules.description: Prevents Title Case formatting in headlines — all headlines must use sentence case only
rules.rationale: Tyroola brand guidelines mandate sentence case in all ad headlines for consistency, readability, and a conversational tone. Title Case reads as generic and outdated in digital advertising contexts.

rules.name: fabricated_stats_protection
rules.description: Prevents use of fabricated statistics or customer counts that are not in facts.json. Verified claims like 'save up to 50%' and '4.7 stars' are allowed.
rules.rationale: Only block fabricated statistics — not verified claims. Tyroola's verified fact 'save up to 50% off RRP' (SV-001) and brand-specific promotions are legitimate. Patterns for price anchors (from $X, save $X) and verified stats (rated 4.7, 800+ locations) have been removed.

rules.name: geographic_service_area_accuracy
rules.description: Prevents claims of service coverage in areas or countries where Tyroola mobile fitting is not available
rules.rationale: Mobile tyre fitting is available across 800+ locations but not everywhere. Overstating coverage creates customer disappointment and potential misleading conduct claims under Australian Consumer Law.

rules.name: regulatory_compliance_safety_claims
rules.description: Prevents unsubstantiated safety endorsements, fake certification claims, or misleading roadworthiness representations
rules.rationale: False or unsubstantiated regulatory endorsement claims violate Australian Consumer Law sections on misleading conduct and false representations. Tyre compliance is a manufacturer responsibility, not a retailer certification.

rules.name: unapproved_payment_providers
rules.description: Prevents naming payment providers not confirmed as current Tyroola partners. Tyre brand names are allowed — Tyroola stocks and promotes them.
rules.rationale: Only verified payment partners (Afterpay, Zip Pay, PayPal Pay in 4) should be named. Tyre brand names (Bridgestone, Kumho, Hankook etc) are legitimate — Tyroola stocks these brands and runs co-branded promotions.

rules.name: misleading_delivery_speed_claims
rules.description: Prevents unverified or absolute delivery and fitting speed promises that cannot be guaranteed for all orders
rules.rationale: Delivery and fitting timeframes depend on location, stock availability, and fitter schedules. Absolute speed promises that cannot be universally fulfilled constitute misleading conduct under Australian Consumer Law.

rules.name: diy_installation_implication_ban
rules.description: Prevents copy that implies customers self-install tyres, which contradicts Tyroola's professional mobile fitting service model
rules.rationale: Tyroola's value proposition includes professional mobile fitting. Implying self-installation undermines the service offering, creates safety concerns, and misrepresents the product to consumers.

rules.name: fabricated_scarcity_ban
rules.description: Prevents fabricated stock scarcity claims. Legitimate time-bound promotions (e.g. 'limited time only' for real sales) are allowed.
rules.rationale: Fabricated stock scarcity is a manipulative dark pattern. However, legitimate promotional urgency ('limited time only' for real seasonal sales) is standard retail practice and drives Tyroola's highest-revenue ads.

rules.name: prohibited_environmental_greenwashing
rules.description: Prevents unsubstantiated environmental or sustainability claims that constitute greenwashing
rules.rationale: Unverified environmental claims are increasingly regulated by the ACCC under its greenwashing enforcement priorities. Tyroola must not make sustainability claims without verified data and certification to support them.

rules.name: convenience_signal_present
rules.description: Ad copy should mention at least one core convenience signal: mobile fitting, home delivery, or online ordering. This is Tyroola's primary value prop and top revenue driver.
rules.rationale: Tyroola's convenience-ease ads generate the highest revenue. Every ad should reinforce at least one differentiator: mobile fitting, home delivery, or the ease of online ordering.

rules.name: value_anchor_present
rules.description: Ad copy should include a value anchor: price match, savings claim, or buy-now-pay-later mention.
rules.rationale: Price-value is Tyroola's second-highest revenue angle. Anchoring on savings, price match guarantee, or BNPL options reduces purchase friction.
